title: Init creates global and project config by default
description: Black-box smoke for `armature init` in a clean temporary project and home.
input: Run `armature init` in an empty temporary project with an isolated HOME, verify both config files are created, and confirm the user-facing next steps are printed.
command: bash -lc 'REPO_ROOT=${PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}; cd "$REPO_ROOT"; bash agent-eval/scripts/prepare-fast-gate.sh >/dev/null; TMP_HOME=$(mktemp -d); TMP_PROJECT=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP_HOME $TMP_PROJECT" EXIT; OUTPUT=$(cd "$TMP_PROJECT" && env -i PATH="$PATH" HOME="$TMP_HOME" ARMATURE_HOME="$TMP_HOME/.armature" node "$REPO_ROOT/dist/bin/armature.js" init); printf "%s\n" "$OUTPUT"; test -f "$TMP_HOME/.armature/config.json"; test -f "$TMP_PROJECT/.armature.json"; printf "init-created-ok\n"'
